"vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Summary

`python-multipart` has a denial of service vulnerability in multipart part header parsing. When parsing `multipart/form-data`, `MultipartParser` previously had no limit on the number of part headers or the size of an individual part header. An attacker could send a request with either many repeated headers without terminating the header block or a single very large header value, causing excessive CPU work before request rejection or completion.

### Impact

Applications that parse attacker-controlled `multipart/form-data` with affected versions of `python-multipart` can experience CPU exhaustion. ASGI applications using Starlette, FastAPI, or other frameworks that invoke `python-multipart` may have worker or event-loop delays while processing malicious upload requests.

### Details

The affected parser states are `HEADER_FIELD_START`, `HEADER_FIELD`, `HEADER_VALUE_START`, `HEADER_VALUE`, and `HEADER_VALUE_ALMOST_DONE`. The issue can be triggered by:

- A multipart part with an oversized individual header value.
- A multipart part with many repeated header lines or an unterminated header block.

Both variants are addressed by enforcing default parser limits for maximum header count and maximum header size.

### Mitigation

Upgrade to `python-multipart` `0.0.27` or later.

If upgrading is not immediately possible, reduce exposure by enforcing request body size limits at the server, proxy, or framework layer. This is only a mitigation; affected versions of `python-multipart` still parse multipart part headers without the default header count and header size limits.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00549, "percentile": 0.41762}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-pp6c-gr5w-3c5g", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-06T21:56:14Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-pp6c-gr5w-3c5g", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-pp6c-gr5w-3c5g"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-42561"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-13T21:16:47Z", "published_at": "2026-05-06T21:56:14Z", "references": ["https://github.com/Kludex/python-multipart/security/advisories/GHSA-pp6c-gr5w-3c5g", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42561", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-pp6c-gr5w-3c5g"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/Kludex/python-multipart/security-advisories/GHSA-pp6c-gr5w-3c5g", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/Kludex/python-multipart", "summary": "python-multipart has Denial of Service via unbounded multipart part headers", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-14T20:42:20Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-pp6c-gr5w-3c5g", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.0.27", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "python-multipart"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 0.0.27"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: python-multipart has Denial of Service via unbounded multipart part headers
